decisions.question: Where in the pipeline does vault:// URI resolution happen relative to parse and merge, and what are the boundaries between the config, resolution, and merge stages?
decisions.chosen: Separate Resolver stage between load and merge (vault.ResolveWorkspace / vault.ResolveGlobalOverride run in apply.go between parse and MergeGlobalOverride; fields carry a MaybeSecret sum type post-resolve)
decisions.rationale: D-6 mandates resolve-before-merge to honor D-9 file-local scoping. Architect review M-1/M-2 flagged this as the central structural question: naive parse->merge->resolve flattens provider origins, breaking file-local scoping. The design must commit to exact stage boundaries (does resolution happen inside LoadWorkspace, in a separate Resolver, or as a pre-merge hook?), what the merge pipeline sees after resolution (typed secret.Value vs URI strings), and how v0.6 configs without vaults pass through unchanged.

decisions.question: What is the shape of the secret.Value type, and how does redaction survive formatters, JSON encoding, gob, error-wrap chains (fmt.Errorf %w), and captured provider-CLI stderr?
decisions.chosen: Struct wrapper secret.Value + secret.Error wrapper + context-scoped Redactor (secret.Wrap / secret.Errorf); plaintext access only via internal/secret/reveal.UnsafeReveal; go/analysis linter deferred to post-v1
decisions.rationale: R22 is load-bearing for most of the eleven invariants. PRD specifies behaviors (%s, %v, %+v, %q, MarshalJSON, MarshalText, GobEncoder all emit ***) and requires error-wrap coverage plus acceptance test for provider-CLI stderr scrub. Architect review S-2 flagged the under-specification. Options span type shape (struct wrapper, string alias, interface), wrap strategy (secret.Error wrapper type, sentinel wrapping, opaque error kind), and enforcement (lint, tests, compile-time traps).

decisions.question: What exactly is the Provider Go interface surface, and how does it admit both stateful (Infisical session-caching) and stateless (sops) backends without forcing one pattern?
decisions.chosen: Factory + Provider + optional BatchResolver, with typed Ref and VersionToken structs, Registry indexed by kind (new internal/vault/ package)
decisions.rationale: R1 skeleton is Resolve(ctx, ref) -> (secret.Value, VersionToken, error) plus Close() plus Name()/Kind(). The design must decide on: VersionToken shape (opaque string, structured type), Ref type (raw URI or parsed struct), whether sessions are init'd lazily or at registration, how subprocess stderr is scrubbed at the interface boundary, and batching admissibility (does the interface need a Resolve-many for performance).

decisions.question: What is the SourceFingerprint reduction shape and state-file storage strategy (rollup hash only vs per-source tuple list vs both)?
decisions.chosen: Rollup hash + tuple list, both stored inline in state.json (SchemaVersion bumps 1 to 2, additive)
decisions.rationale: R15 requires SHA-256 of stable-sorted (source-id, version-token) tuples for files that blend multiple sources (the common .local.env case). Architect review M-4 flagged this as underspecified. Options: (a) store only rollup hash (small state, ambiguous per-source changes), (b) store the tuple list too (bigger state, actionable diagnostics), (c) store both with tuple list opt-in. Also open: per-backend version-token provenance shape (git SHA for sops, audit-log pointer for Infisical) and how niwa status surfaces the provenance on 'stale'.

decisions.question: Where does the public-repo guardrail live in the code layering, and how does it detect 'public GitHub remote' (URL pattern vs API call vs explicit flag)?
decisions.chosen: URL pattern match only, in new internal/guardrail/ package (CheckGitHubPublicRemoteSecrets); called from apply.go after vault resolve, before merge
decisions.rationale: R14 requires enumerating all git remotes (not just origin) and blocking apply on plaintext *.secrets values if any remote is public GitHub. Architect review S-1 flagged placement concern: config package must stay ignorant of git. Options for detection: (a) URL-pattern match (cheap, works offline, can't distinguish public from private repos at same host), (b) GitHub API probe (accurate but needs auth), (c) explicit workspace-toml flag (no detection, requires team cooperation). Options for placement: internal/workspace/apply.go, new internal/guardrail/, or config-adjacent.

decisions.question: Where in the pipeline is shadow detection computed for R31 override-visibility, and what is the integration surface between the merge stage, niwa apply stderr, niwa status summary, and niwa status --audit-secrets?
decisions.chosen: Pure visitor walk (DetectProviderShadows at resolve time + DetectShadows post-resolve pre-merge); MergeGlobalOverride signature preserved; Shadows persisted in InstanceState (schema 1 to 2) for niwa status consumption
decisions.rationale: R31 requires stderr diagnostic on apply, shadowed-count summary in niwa status, and shadow flags in --audit-secrets. Three surfaces, one underlying detection. Options: (a) compute in MergeGlobalOverride as a side channel (Shadows []ShadowInfo field), (b) post-merge visitor walk comparing pre/post-merge snapshots, (c) recompute independently in each surface. Must not print shadowed/shadowing values (R22 redaction applies).
